data.title: Epic closed as complete without merging final batch — orchestrator discarded unmerged code on merge conflict
data.description:
## What happened (P0)

A session claimed an epic was complete and recommended discarding the worktree when the final batch of epic changes had NOT been merged into main. The orchestrator encountered merge conflicts and assumed that because conflicts existed, someone else had already completed comparable changes on main. This is incorrect — merge conflicts do not mean the work was duplicated.

## Impact

- Epic marked as complete when it was not
- Critical code changes recommended for discard (worktree cleanup)
- User could lose an entire batch of implemented code

## Root causes

1. **Orchestrator assumed merge conflicts = work already done on main**: When merge-to-main.sh reported conflicts, the orchestrator concluded the work was redundant rather than resolving the conflicts or escalating.

2. **Completion verifier did not check merge status**: The dso:completion-verifier sub-agent verified story completion criteria (tests pass, acceptance criteria met) but did NOT verify that changes were merged to main. An epic cannot be truly complete if its code only exists on an unmerged worktree branch.

3. **End-session flow allowed closing epic without merge confirmation**: The /dso:end-session workflow should block epic closure if the worktree branch has unmerged commits.

## Fix needed

1. dso:completion-verifier MUST verify that changes are merged to main (or at minimum that the worktree branch is ancestor of main) before approving epic closure
2. /dso:end-session MUST NOT recommend discarding a worktree with unmerged commits that are part of the epic
3. Sprint orchestrator MUST NOT interpret merge conflicts as 'work already done' — merge conflicts require resolution, not abandonment
4. Add a HARD-GATE: epic cannot transition to closed while the branch has commits not in main
